responses.question: What is Overfitting?
responses.answer: Overfitting is a problem in machine learning where a model learns the training data to weld, including its noise and random patterns instead of learning the underlying relationships. As a result, it performs very well on the training data but poorly on new unseen data.

similarity.matched_content: In mathematical modeling, overfitting is the production of an analysis that corresponds too closely or exactly to a particular set of data, and may therefore fail to fit to additional data or predict future observations reliably. An overfitted model is a mathematical model that contains more parameters than can be justified by the data. In the special case of a model that consists of a polynomial function, these parameters represent the degree of a polynomial. The essence of overfitting is to unknowingly extract some of the residual variation as if that variation represents the underlying model structure.

retrieved_documents.title: Overfitting
retrieved_documents.content: In mathematical modeling, overfitting is the production of an analysis that corresponds too closely or exactly to a particular set of data, and may therefore fail to fit to additional data or predict future observations reliably. An overfitted model is a mathematical model that contains more parameters than can be justified by the data. In the special case of a model that consists of a polynomial function, these parameters represent the degree of a polynomial. The essence of overfitting is to unknowingly extract some of the residual variation as if that variation represents the underlying model structure.
